pr.title: Axial precession of the Earth
pr.desc: Earth's rotation axis is tilted 23.4 degrees from the ecliptic normal and slowly sweeps a full cone, so the north celestial pole traces a circle against the fixed stars — passing Thuban around 2800 BCE, Polaris today, and Vega around 13,700 CE.

gy.title: The Great Year as a wheel of twelve Ages
gy.desc: The ~25,772-year precessional cycle divided into twelve Ages of about 2,160 years each, one per zodiac sign. The equinox drifts backward through the zodiac at roughly one degree every 72 years and currently sits at the Pisces–Aquarius boundary, entering the Age of Aquarius.
gy.foot: Each Age = one Great Month = 30° of precession ≈ 2,160 years

wh.title: The Wheel of Heaven — twelve precessional ages of the project
wh.desc: The twelve ~2,160-year precessional ages read as phases of the alliance's project, with boundary years marked around the rim: from the Age of Capricorn (~21,810 BCE, project opening) through Leo (humans made), Cancer (Eden), Gemini (the Flood), Taurus (Babel), Aries (the prophets), Pisces (the gospels), to the current Age of Aquarius (from 1950 CE).
wh.foot: Each age ≈ 2,160 years · a full turn ≈ one Great Year

ep.title: The four-tier epistemic distinction
ep.desc: A pyramid of four content tiers the corpus keeps separate: direct source claims at the firm base, then comparative observations, then Wheel of Heaven interpretation, with open questions at the interpretive capstone.

tb.title: The precessional-age framework as a timeline
tb.desc: Twelve contiguous ~2,160-year precessional ages from the Age of Capricorn (~21,810 BCE, project opening) to the Age of Aquarius (from 1950 CE, the current age), each marked with its zodiac glyph.
